description: Negative fixture cases for the security-response packet gate. Each case is a complete packet that is structurally valid except for one targeted flaw; the typed model loads it and asserts validation rejects it.

cases.summary: A response row whose state forces narrowing still carries a Stable lifecycle label instead of narrowing below the cutline.
A ready response row rides a response packet whose freshness-SLO state is breached, so the packet-freshness automation must narrow it before publication.
A ready emergency-disable row carries an unsatisfied required emergency control, so it must narrow before publication.
A ready mirror/offline-drill row carries an unverified required checkpoint, so it must narrow before publication.
A gap reason has no corresponding response rule watching for it, so a gap could fire without a publication gate.
A response row's effective label is wider than the public claim's canonical ceiling label.
A narrowing row with a breached packet does not name the matching freshness-breach reason.
